channel.title: EDGAR Live Scanner
channel.description: High-signal SEC EDGAR filings — tender offers, going-private, activist stakes, spinoffs, delistings, busted deals.

item.title: 🤝 Merger · PUK
item.ns0:headline: Acquires 75% of Bharti Life Insurance for $389M cash; regulatory approvals pending
item.description:
Acquires 75% of Bharti Life Insurance for $389M cash; regulatory approvals pending

Agrees to acquire a 75% controlling stake in Bharti Life Insurance Company Limited for an initial cash consideration of ₹3,500 crore (~$389 million), with potential additional consideration of up to ₹700 crore (~$78 million) subject to conditions. Completion is subject to regulatory approvals, which are expected to require Prudential to reduce its shareholding in ICICI Prudential Life Insurance to under 10%. The transaction will be funded from existing resources; no specific closing timeline provided.

About: Prudential plc, through its subsidiaries, provides life and health insurance, and retirement and asset management solutions to individuals in Asia, and Africa. It offers health and protection, as well as savings products, such as participating, linked, and other traditional products. The company also provides insurance against common critical illnesses, including cancer, stroke, and heart attack; and tropical disease protection, such as dengue, malaria, and measles.

  • Counterparty: Bharti Life Insurance Company Limited
  • Deal value: $389 million (initial)
  • Stake: 75%
  • Consideration: cash
  • Announced: 2026-05-18

Px $31.46 · Mkt cap $39.83B · EV $28.55B · Fwd P/E 12.8x · Fwd EV/EBITDA 22.9x · EV/Sales 4.2x · Financial Services / Insurance - Life

The Terminals and Infrastructure segment engages in the natural gas procurement and liquefaction; and shipping, logistics, facilities and conversion, or development of natural gas-fired power generation.

item.title: 🎯 Tender · ASRT
item.ns0:headline: Acquired by Zydus for $23.50/share cash; tender offer commenced May 18
item.description:
Acquired by Zydus for $23.50/share cash; tender offer commenced May 18

Entered into a merger agreement with Zydus Worldwide DMCC for $23.50 per share in cash via a tender offer for all outstanding common stock. The tender offer commenced on May 18, 2026, and will be followed by a merger where Assertio becomes a wholly owned subsidiary of Zydus. Holders of Assertio's 6.50% Convertible Senior Notes due 2027 will have rights to require repurchase or convert into merger consideration.

About: Assertio Holdings, Inc., a specialty pharmaceutical company, provides medicines in the areas of neurology, hospital, and pain and inflammation. Its pharmaceutical products include INDOCIN, an oral solution and a suppository form for the treatment of moderate to severe rheumatoid arthritis, including acute flares of chronic disease; ankylosing spondylitis and osteoarthritis; and acute painful shoulder and gouty arthritis.

  • Counterparty: Zydus Worldwide DMCC
  • Offer price: $23.50
  • Consideration: cash
  • Announced: 2026-05-13

item.title: 🤝 Merger · QNCX
item.ns0:headline: Acquires Orphai in stock merger; pre-deal holders retain ~17.8% stake
item.description:
Acquires Orphai in stock merger; pre-deal holders retain ~17.8% stake

Acquired Orphai Therapeutics in a stock-for-stock merger, issuing 3,258,517 common shares and 67,101.235 Series C convertible preferred shares (each convertible into 1,000 common shares). Pre-transaction Quince shareholders hold approximately 17.8% of the combined company on a fully-diluted basis (6.9% after a concurrent financing), while former Orphai holders hold 82.2% (31.9% after financing). The board unanimously approved; a stockholders' meeting will be held to approve conversion of preferred stock and a charter amendment to increase authorized shares to 800 million.

About: Quince Therapeutics, Inc., a biopharmaceutical company, focuses on advancing precision therapeutics for debilitating and rare diseases. The company has discovered a broad bone-targeting drug platform to precisely deliver small molecules, peptides, or large molecules directly to the site of bone fracture and disease. Its lead compound is NOV004, an anabolic peptide engineered to precisely target and concentrate at the bone fracture site The company was formerly known as Cortexyme, Inc.

  • Counterparty: Orphai Therapeutics
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2026-05-17

Px $1.17 · Mkt cap $6.5M · EV $180.4M · Healthcare / Biotechnology

PAD shareholders will receive a mix of cash and shares of New PAD common stock; SPAC shareholders have redemption rights at ~$10.59 per share. The deal requires shareholder approval and a minimum of $75M in cash at closing.

The company intends to effect a merger, share exchange, asset acquisition, share purchase, reorganization, or related business combination with one or more businesses. The company was incorporated in 2020 and is based in New York, New York.

item.description:
Self-tender for Series A/B preferred at $22.50/sh; up to $100M; expires June 16

Is offering to purchase up to $100 million of its Series A and Series B perpetual preferred shares at $22.50 per share in cash. The tender offer expires June 16, 2026; Series A shares have purchase priority over Series B, and odd-lot holders receive priority within each series. The board makes no recommendation; the price is below the liquidation preference of $25.00 per share.

About: National Healthcare Properties, Inc. is a real estate investment trust, which engages in acquiring, owning, and managing a diversified portfolio of healthcare real estate assets. It operates through the following segments: Outpatient Medical Facilities (OMF) and Senior Housing Operating Properties (SHOP).

item.title: 🤝 Merger · SLNO
item.ns0:headline: Acquired by Neurocrine for $53/share cash; merger closed May 18
item.description:
Acquired by Neurocrine for $53/share cash; merger closed May 18

Neurocrine Biosciences completed its acquisition of Soleno Therapeutics via a tender offer and subsequent merger under DGCL 251(h). Holders received $53.00 per share in cash; 88.9% of shares were tendered. The merger closed on May 18, 2026, and Soleno became a wholly owned subsidiary of Neurocrine.

About: Soleno Therapeutics, Inc., a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, focuses on the development and commercialization of novel therapeutics for the treatment of rare diseases. Its lead candidate is Diazoxide Choline Controlled-Release, a once-daily oral tablet for the treatment of Prader-Willi Syndrome, which is being evaluated in an ongoing Phase III clinical development program. The company was formerly known as Capnia, Inc. and changed its name to Soleno Therapeutics, Inc. in May 2017.

  • Counterparty: Neurocrine Biosciences, Inc.
  • Offer price: 53.00
  • Consideration: cash
  • Announced: 2026-04-05
  • Expected Close: 2026-05-18

Px $53.01 · Mkt cap $2.76B · EV $2.39B · Fwd P/E 13.4x · Fwd EV/EBITDA 6.2x · EV/Sales 5.1x · Healthcare / Biotechnology

item.description:

Entered into Amendment No. 1 to its merger agreement with Ligand Pharmaceuticals, adding newly formed HoldCo as a party. The merger, originally announced April 27, 2026, remains subject to stockholder approval and other conditions. A definitive proxy statement will be filed and mailed to stockholders.

About: XOMA Royalty Corp. operates as a biotechnology royalty aggregator in Europe, the United States, and the Asia Pacific. The company engages in helping biotech companies for enhancing human health. It acquires the potential future economics associated with pre-commercial therapeutic candidates that have been licensed to pharmaceutical or biotechnology companies.

item.title: 🤝 Merger · NBIX
item.ns0:headline: Acquires Soleno Therapeutics for $53/share cash; merger closed
item.description:
Acquires Soleno Therapeutics for $53/share cash; merger closed

Completed its acquisition of Soleno Therapeutics for $53.00 per share in cash, representing an aggregate value of approximately $2.9 billion. The tender offer expired on May 15, 2026, with 88.9% of Soleno shares tendered, and the merger closed on May 18, 2026 under Section 251(h) of the DGCL. Soleno became a wholly owned subsidiary of Neurocrine.

About: Neurocrine Biosciences, Inc. discovers, develops, and markets pharmaceuticals for neurological, endocrine, and psychiatric disorders. The company's portfolio includes treatments for tardive dyskinesia, Parkinson's disease, endometriosis, and uterine fibroids, as well as clinical programs in various therapeutic areas. Its lead asset is INGREZZA, a VMAT2 inhibitor for the treatment of tardive dyskinesia.

  • Offer price: $53.00
  • Deal value: $2.9 billion
  • Consideration: cash
  • Announced: 2026-04-05
  • Expected Close: 2026-05-18

item.ns0:headline: Nasdaq minimum bid price deficiency; 180-day cure until Nov 11, 2026
item.description:
Nasdaq minimum bid price deficiency; 180-day cure until Nov 11, 2026

Received a Nasdaq notice for non-compliance with the $1.00 minimum bid price requirement. The company has 180 calendar days, until November 11, 2026, to regain compliance by achieving a closing bid price of at least $1.00 for 10 consecutive business days. If not cured, it may qualify for an additional 180-day period or face delisting.

item.title: 🤝 Merger · SLNO
item.ns0:headline: Acquires Soleno for $53/share cash; 88.9% tendered; merger closed May 18
item.description:
Acquires Soleno for $53/share cash; 88.9% tendered; merger closed May 18

Neurocrine Biosciences completed its acquisition of Soleno Therapeutics via a cash tender offer at $53.00 per share, representing a total equity value of $2.9 billion. As of the May 15 expiration, 46,356,114 shares (88.9% of outstanding) were validly tendered; all conditions were satisfied, and the merger was effected under Section 251(h) of the DGCL on May 18, 2026. Soleno's common stock will be delisted from Nasdaq.

About: Soleno Therapeutics, Inc., a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, focuses on the development and commercialization of novel therapeutics for the treatment of rare diseases. Its lead candidate is Diazoxide Choline Controlled-Release, a once-daily oral tablet for the treatment of Prader-Willi Syndrome, which is being evaluated in an ongoing Phase III clinical development program. The company was formerly known as Capnia, Inc. and changed its name to Soleno Therapeutics, Inc. in May 2017.

  • Counterparty: Neurocrine Biosciences, Inc.
  • Offer price: $53.00
  • Deal value: $2.9 billion
  • Stake: 88.9%
  • Consideration: cash
  • Announced: 2026-04-06
  • Expected Close: 2026-05-18

Acquires 100% of Omnisys Ltd., an Israeli defense software company, for $199 million in common stock (subject to adjustments) plus up to $60 million in earn-out. The purchase price includes $29M at closing, $142.5M in five equal installments post-closing, and the balance on the 24th trading day after closing. The acquisition is expected to close in Q2 2026, subject to Omnisys shareholder consent, governmental approvals, and other conditions.

About: Ondas Holdings Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides private wireless, drone, and automated data solutions. The company operates in two segments, Ondas Networks and American Robotics. The company designs, develops, manufactures, sells, and supports FullMAX, a software defined radio (SDR) platform for wide-area broadband networks. Its FullMAX SDR platform enables secure and reliable industrial-grade connectivity for truly mission-critical applications.

item.title: 🤝 Merger · PBT
item.ns0:headline: Proposed merger with Blackbeard; PBT unitholders to own 58% of New PubCo
item.description:
Proposed merger with Blackbeard; PBT unitholders to own 58% of New PubCo

SoftVest (minority unitholder) and Blackbeard signed a non-binding term sheet for a business combination where Permian Basin Royalty Trust's assets and Blackbeard's US Land Guild (66,500 acres, 15% royalty) would be contributed to New PubCo. PBT unitholders would receive ~58% of New PubCo stock, Blackbeard ~42%, and New PubCo would assume ~$80M debt. The transaction is subject to definitive agreements, regulatory approvals, and unitholder vote; no definitive agreement yet.

About: Permian Basin Royalty Trust, an express trust, holds overriding royalty interests in various oil and gas properties in the United States. The company owns a 75% net overriding royalty interest in the Waddell Ranch properties comprising Dune, Sand Hills (Judkins), Sand Hills (McKnight), Sand Hills (Tubb), University-Waddell (Devonian), and Waddell fields located in Crane County, Texas.

  • Counterparty: Blackbeard Holdings, LLC
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2026-05-18

The company is urging shareholders to vote the WHITE card for its three nominees (Chip Bergh, Esi Eggleston Bracey, Teri List), arguing Wilson's nominees lack relevant experience and his campaign is driven by personal grievances. Filed Form 15-12G to terminate its registration under Section 12(g) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, effective immediately. The company has approximately four holders of record, falling below the threshold requiring SEC reporting. As a result, the company will cease filing periodic reports with the SEC, though its stock may continue to trade on OTC markets as a non-reporting entity.

The company offers body and facial waxing services; and pre- and post-service products, including ingrown hair serums, exfoliating gels, brow shapers, and skin treatments. As of March 26, 2022, it had a portfolio of centers operating in 874 locations across 44 states, including 868 franchised centers and six corporate-owned centers. European Wax Center, Inc.

item.title: ⚖️ Litigation · GPRO
item.ns0:headline: Court invalidates patent claim, vacates $8.2M damages; GoPro has no liability
item.description:
Court invalidates patent claim, vacates $8.2M damages; GoPro has no liability

The U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California granted GoPro's motion for judgment as a matter of law, invalidating the sole remaining patent claim (claim 11 of U.S. Patent No. 8,890,954) as obvious. The court vacated the $8.2 million damages award from the October 2025 jury verdict and denied all of Contour IP Holding's post-trial motions. GoPro has no liability from the case, though the ruling is subject to appeal to the Federal Circuit.

About: GoPro, Inc. engages in manufacturing and selling cameras and camera accessories. It provides mountable and wearable cameras and accessories, which it refers to as capture devices. Its product brands include HERO9 Black, HERO8 Black, Max, HERO7 Black, HERO7 Silver, GoPro Plus, and GoPro App. The company was founded by Nicholas Woodman in 2002 and is headquartered in San Mateo, CA.

  • Counterparty: Contour IP Holding LLC
  • Announced: 2026-05-14

item.title: 🤝 Merger · SACH
item.ns0:headline: IRG Global to contribute $2.9B portfolio for 94.1% control of Sachem; reverse split 20:1
item.description:
IRG Global to contribute $2.9B portfolio for 94.1% control of Sachem; reverse split 20:1

Entered into a Contribution Agreement with Industrial Realty Group Global, LLC, under which IRG Global will contribute its industrial real estate portfolio (implied gross asset value ~$2.9B, net asset value ~$1.5B) to a new operating partnership in exchange for OP Units and Class B shares representing 94.1% of OP Units and 51% voting power. Sachem will redomesticate to Delaware, effect a 20-to-1 reverse stock split, rename to IRG Realty Trust, Inc., and become a REIT. Closing expected by end of 2026; Sachem's current shareholders will hold approximately 5.9% of OP Units post-closing.

About: Sachem Capital Corp. operates as a real estate finance company. The company is involved in the originating, underwriting, funding, servicing, and managing a portfolio of short-term loans secured by first mortgage liens on real property located primarily in Northeastern United States and Florida. It offers loans to real estate investors and owners to fund their acquisition, renovation, rehabilitation, development, and/or improvement of residential or commercial properties.

  • Counterparty: Industrial Realty Group Global, LLC
  • Deal value: $2.9 billion (gross asset value)
  • Stake: 94.1%
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2026-05-17
  • Expected Close: end of 2026

item.description:

Entered into a Contribution Agreement with Industrial Realty Group Global to contribute IRG's industrial real estate portfolio (98 assets, ~$2.9B gross asset value) in exchange for ~94.1% of the combined company's OP units and Class B shares with 51% voting power. Sachem existing shareholders will retain ~5.9% ownership; the transaction implies a $2.00 per share value for Sachem common stock, a 90% premium to the 30-day VWAP. The deal is subject to Sachem shareholder approval, regulatory conditions, and pre-closing reorganization including a 20-to-1 reverse stock split, redomestication to Delaware, and name change to IRG Realty Trust; closing expected by end of 2026.

item.description:

Is asking shareholders to vote FOR Resolution 25, a waiver of mandatory offer provisions under Rule 9 of the UK Takeover Code, to allow continuation of its €1bn share buyback program announced Feb 17, 2026. ISS recommends against the waiver, but Glass Lewis recommends FOR; the AGM is on May 28, 2026.

  • Consideration: cash

Exchanged approximately $252.5M of outstanding promissory notes for non-convertible, non-voting preferred stock and issued $30M of Series D preferred stock and warrants to affiliates of Chicago Pacific Founders. The transactions were completed to regain compliance with Nasdaq's minimum stockholders' equity requirement of $2.5M; the company believes it now complies but Nasdaq will monitor. item.description:

Received FDA accelerated approval for BEQALZI (sonrotoclax) for relapsed/refractory mantle cell lymphoma after ≥2 prior therapies including a BTK inhibitor. Approval based on Phase 1/2 study showing 52% ORR, 16% CR, median DOR 15.8 months. Continued approval contingent on confirmatory CELESTIAL-RRMCL trial.

item.ns0:headline: Vote 'no' campaign against Chair Donna James; annual meeting June 11
item.description:
Vote 'no' campaign against Chair Donna James; annual meeting June 11

BBRC International PTE Limited, holding 13.0% of Victoria's Secret & Co., is soliciting votes against the election of Chair Donna James at the 2026 annual meeting on June 11, citing financial underperformance, governance concerns, and a 25-year tenure. The activist is not nominating a slate; the election is uncontested but subject to a majority voting standard. BBRC has engaged with the board since 2022 and previously sought board representation.

About: Victoria's Secret & Co. operates as a specialty retailer of women's intimate, personal care, and beauty products worldwide. The company offers bras, panties, lingerie, sleepwear, loungewear, and athletic attire and swimwear, as well as fragrances and body care products, and accessories under the Victoria's Secret and PINK brands. As of March 2, 2022, it operated approximately 1,400 retail stores. The company was incorporated in 2021 and is headquartered in Reynoldsburg, Ohio.

Completed its acquisition of Owen Mumford, a UK-based medical device company, for £100 million upfront cash (subject to customary adjustments) and up to £50 million in milestone payments based on net sales of the Aidaptus auto-injector platform through June 30, 2029. About: Embecta Corp., a medical device company, focuses on the provision of various solutions to enhance the health and wellbeing of people living with diabetes. Shareholder George Assad filed a lawsuit alleging the proxy statement for Two Harbors' merger with CrossCountry Intermediate Holdco (CCM) is materially incomplete and misleading. The suit seeks to enjoin the May 19 stockholder vote until corrective disclosures are made; a TRO hearing is set for May 18. Two Harbors denies merit but disclosed the complaint to avoid delay and litigation expense.

About: Two Harbors Investment Corp. operates as a real estate investment trust (REIT) that focuses on investing in, financing, and managing residential mortgage-backed securities (RMBS), non-agency securities, mortgage servicing rights, and other financial assets in the United States. Its target assets include agency RMBS collateralized by fixed rate mortgage loans, adjustable rate mortgage loans, and hybrid adjustable-rate mortgage (ARMs); and other assets, such as financial and mortgage-related asset…

item.title: 🤝 Merger · EBAY
item.ns0:headline: Bids $55B for eBay; 46% premium; Cohen defends financing
item.description:
Bids $55B for eBay; 46% premium; Cohen defends financing

GameStop Corp., led by Ryan Cohen, made an unsolicited $55 billion bid for eBay, offering half cash and half stock, representing a 46% premium to eBay's price when GameStop began accumulating shares. eBay's board rejected the proposal as not credible, citing financing uncertainty and operational risks. Cohen defends the bid, citing $9.4B cash on hand, up to $20B in debt financing from TD Securities, and $2B in planned cost cuts; the bid remains outstanding.

About: eBay Inc. operates marketplace platforms that connect buyers and sellers in the United States and internationally. The company's Marketplace platform includes its online marketplace at ebay.com and the eBay suite of mobile apps.   • Premium: 46

item.title: ⚔️ Proxy Contest · FRMI
item.ns0:headline: Nominates 4-director slate; special meeting May 29 after court win
item.description:
Nominates 4-director slate; special meeting May 29 after court win

Toby Neugebauer, Fermi's largest shareholder, is soliciting proxies to elect his slate of four director nominees at the May 29 special meeting. The court denied Fermi's attempt to block the meeting, but the board adopted defensive bylaw amendments raising the director-election threshold to 70%. Neugebauer advocates for a sale or strategic partnership to maximize value.

About: Fermi, Inc. engages in the development of energy infrastructure. It intends to develop an energy and data center development campus to support the needs of to-be-built AI infrastructure. The company was founded by Rick Perry, Toby Neugebauer and Griffin Perry on January 10, 2025 and is headquartered in Amrillo, TX.

item.ns0:headline: Offers $2.40/share (42% premium) + CVR; nominates 3 directors
item.description:
Offers $2.40/share (42% premium) + CVR; nominates 3 directors

The Radoff-JEC Group, owning ~7.8% of Seer, submitted a third non-binding proposal to acquire the company for $2.40 per share in cash plus a contingent value right (CVR) entitling stockholders to 80% of net proceeds from any sale of Seer's assets. The offer represents a 42% premium to the unaffected share price and expires May 29, 2026. Simultaneously, the group is nominating three independent director candidates (Howard H. Berman, Joshua S. Horowitz, Luis E. Rinaldini) for election at the upcoming annual meeting, and intends to file a proxy statement to solicit votes.

About: Seer, Inc., a life sciences company, engages in developing and commercializing products to decode the secrets of the proteome. Entered into a Third Amendment to its merger agreement with BOXABL Inc., revising lock-up terms for company and sponsor shares. Under the amended lock-up, 50% of company shares are released 6 months post-close if the stock price meets or exceeds $12.00 for 20 of 30 trading days, with the remainder released at 13 months; sponsor shares have a 12-month release with similar $12 threshold. Full early release occurs if the price reaches $20.00 intraday. The merger remains subject to shareholder vote and regulatory approvals.

item.title: ⚖️ Litigation · MBRX
item.ns0:headline: Phase 2B/3 MIRACLE trial unblinding expected before June 30; blinded CR rate ~30% vs 17-18% historical
item.description:
Phase 2B/3 MIRACLE trial unblinding expected before June 30; blinded CR rate ~30% vs 17-18% historical

Announced imminent unblinding of first 45 subjects from its pivotal Phase 2B/3 MIRACLE trial evaluating Annamycin + cytarabine in relapsed/refractory AML. Preliminary blinded data show a complete remission rate of approximately 30%, compared to historical 17-18% for cytarabine alone. Unblinding is expected before June 30, 2026, with Part A recruitment on track to reach 90 subjects in Q3 2026.

About: Moleculin Biotech, Inc., a clinical-stage pharmaceutical company, focuses on the development of drug candidates for the treatment of highly resistant tumors and viruses. Its lead drug candidate is Annamycin that is in Phase 1/2 studies for the treatment of relapsed or refractory acute myeloid leukemia (AML) and cancers metastasized to the lungs.

  • Announced: 2026-05-13

item.description:

Entered a Fifth Forbearance Agreement with lender J.J. Astor & Co., modifying repayment terms on the outstanding Second Note ($5,995,722). The agreement includes a new $1,500,000 convertible note (the Fifth Note) with proceeds of $1,050,000, and issuance of 1,000,000 commitment shares. Repayment schedule extended to January 1, 2027, with weekly installments starting at $50,000 and increasing to $250,000. The company remains in default but avoids acceleration.

About: Vivakor, Inc. operates, acquires, and develops clean energy technologies and environmental solutions primarily focused on soil remediation in the United States and Kuwait. The company was founded in 2006 and is based in Lehi, Utah.

item.ns0:headline: Saba proxy contest for ECAT board; vote pending
item.description:
Saba proxy contest for ECAT board; vote pending

Saba Capital is waging a proxy contest for board seats at BlackRock ESG Capital Allocation Term Trust (ECAT). Saba has been actively selling its ECAT position while seeking board representation. The ECAT Board is soliciting shareholder support, highlighting the fund's performance and discount management. Saba previously settled with 50 other BlackRock CEFs but excluded ECAT. The annual meeting vote is pending.

Pacific Booker Minerals Inc. was incorporated in 1983 and is headquartered in Vancouver, Canada.

item.description:
Sells Transportation & Vendor Finance businesses to Stonepeak for cash + earnout; retains 19.9% stake

BMO signed a definitive agreement to sell its Transportation Finance and Vendor Finance businesses (combined loan/lease portfolio of ~C$14.5B) to Stonepeak for cash consideration plus an earnout. BMO will retain a 19.9% equity interest in the new entity. The transaction is expected to close in Q4 fiscal 2026, subject to regulatory approvals, and is expected to improve BMO's CET1 ratio by ~28 bps.

item.title: 📉 Delisting · Forced · LNAI
item.ns0:headline: Nasdaq staff determination April 28; seeks extension to June 1
item.description:
Nasdaq staff determination April 28; seeks extension to June 1

Received a Nasdaq staff determination letter on April 28, 2026, for non-compliance with the $35M market value of listed securities rule (MVLS Rule). The company completed a merger on May 1, 2026, issuing $20M Series B Preferred to address equity compliance, and stockholders approved a reverse stock split (1:3 to 1:30) on May 8, 2026, to regain bid price compliance. The Nasdaq Hearings Panel previously granted an exception through June 1, 2026, for bid price compliance, and the company is requesting an extension.

The board urges shareholders to vote for all its nominees, citing strong performance and the risks of disruption. item.title: ⚖️ Litigation · Fda Pre Bla Meeting Positive · MLTX
item.ns0:headline: FDA aligns on sonelokimab BLA for HS; submission Sept 2026, launch H2 2027
item.description:
FDA aligns on sonelokimab BLA for HS; submission Sept 2026, launch H2 2027

Announced positive outcome from final pre-BLA meeting with FDA for sonelokimab (SLK) in hidradenitis suppurativa (HS). FDA aligned on submission plans and label strategy, including acceptability of MIRA trial data for effectiveness and inclusion of adolescent data from VELA-TEEN. BLA submission planned for end of September 2026; acceptance and Priority Review decision expected by end of November 2026; first commercial launch targeted for H2 2027 if approved. Company also reported Q1 2026 cash of $357.9M.

About: MoonLake Immunotherapeutics, a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, engages in developing therapies. It is developing Sonelokimab, a novel investigational Nanobody therapy for the treatment of inflammation. The company is involved in conducting Phase II trials for hidradenitis suppurativa, psoriatic arthritis, ankylosing spondylitis, or radiographic axial spondyloarthritis. MoonLake Immunotherapeutics was founded in 2021 and is headquartered in Zug, Switzerland.

  • Announced: 2026-05-10

item.title: 📉 Delisting · Forced · ADTX
item.ns0:headline: Forced Nasdaq delisting May 6; appeal hearing requested
item.description:
Forced Nasdaq delisting May 6; appeal hearing requested

Received a Staff Determination from Nasdaq on May 6, 2026, that its common stock will be delisted from The Nasdaq Capital Market due to the bid price closing below $1.00 per share for 30 consecutive business days (March 24, 2026 through May 5, 2026). The company is ineligible for a 180-day compliance period because it has effected reverse stock splits over the prior two years with a cumulative ratio of 250 shares or more to one. Aditxt intends to timely request a hearing before a Nasdaq Hearings Panel to appeal the determination, which will stay delisting pending the hearing outcome.

About: Aditxt, Inc., a biotech company, develops technologies focuses on improving the health of the immune system through immune mapping and reprogramming. It develops AditxtScore that allows individuals to understand, manage, and monitor their immune profiles in order to be informed about attacks on or by their immune system; and Apoptotic DNA Immunotherapy, a nucleic acid-based technology that utilizes an approach that mimics the way the body naturally induces tolerance to its own tissues.

  • Announced: 2026-05-06

item.ns0:headline: TDS proposes on May 7 to acquire remaining 18.1% of AD; special committee formed
item.description:
TDS proposes on May 7 to acquire remaining 18.1% of AD; special committee formed

Telephone and Data Systems, Inc. (TDS), which already owns 81.9% of the outstanding stock and 95.9% of the voting interests, submitted a non-binding proposal on May 7, 2026 to acquire all remaining common shares of Array Digital Infrastructure. Array's board formed a special committee of independent directors, which retained PJT Partners as financial advisor and Cravath, Swaine & Moore as legal counsel, to evaluate the proposal. No price or definitive terms have been disclosed, and there is no assurance a transaction will occur.

About: Array Digital Infrastructure, Inc. provides wireless telecommunications services in the United States. The company offers wireless services, including voice, messaging, and data services. It also provides wireless devices, such as handsets, tablets, mobile hotspots, home phones, and routers, as well as wireless essentials, including cases, screen protectors, chargers, and memory cards; and consumer electronics comprising Bluetooth audio, wi-fi enabled cameras, and networking products.

item.title: 🪙 Debt Restr. · Out-Of-Court Amendment · MERC
item.ns0:headline: Amends €370M RCF with UniCredit; waives leverage covenant, caps capex €60M
item.description:
Amends €370M RCF with UniCredit; waives leverage covenant, caps capex €60M

's German subsidiaries entered into a Waiver and Consent Request Letter with lenders to amend their €370.1 million revolving credit facility. The waiver suspends the leverage ratio covenant (max 3.50:1.00) for the first three quarters of fiscal 2026, restricts distributions to the parent (up to €15M aggregate with conditions), caps 2026 capex at €60M, and requires additional security over assets worth at least 110% of drawn amounts. The interest rate margin was modified to 2.50%-4.25% based on leverage levels.

About: Mercer International Inc., together with its subsidiaries, manufactures and sells northern bleached softwood kraft (NBSK) pulp in Europe, the United States, Asia, and internationally. The company operates through two segments, Pulp and Wood Products. It also generates and sells green energy produced from biomass cogeneration power plant to third party utilities. In addition, the company manufactures, distributes, and sells lumber and other wood residuals.

  • Counterparty: UniCredit Bank GmbH (as agent)
  • Deal value: €370,100,000
  • Announced: 2026-05-07

item.description:

Diana Shipping Inc., holder of 14.7% of Genco Shipping & Trading Ltd, is soliciting proxies to elect six director nominees at Genco's 2026 annual meeting on June 18, 2026. Diana, in partnership with Star Bulk, has made an increased offer of $23.50 per share in cash for Genco (31% premium to Nov 21, 2025 close), supported by $1.433 billion in committed financing, but the Genco board has refused to engage. The proxy contest aims to replace the board to facilitate a sale or other value-maximizing transaction.

About: Genco Shipping & Trading Limited, together with its subsidiaries, engages in the ocean transportation of dry bulk cargoes worldwide.
